summary: Russia launched a massive combined airstrike on 24‑25 May, deploying over 690 missiles and Shahed drones targeting Kyiv and multiple regions, with Ukrainian air defenses downing 604 of them. Simultaneously, Russian forces intensified missile strikes on energy infrastructure in Kharkiv, Poltava, Chernihiv and the oil‑gas sector in Kharkiv and Poltava, while Ukrainian forces reported limited tactical gains in Sumy and Kharkiv. Ukraine’s leadership warned of a forthcoming "Orekh"‑type strike and continued to mobilize civil support for defense.
key_points: Russia executed a coordinated strike using 90 rockets and 600 Shahed drones, focusing on Kyiv, with Ukrainian defenses downing 55 rockets and 549 drones (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defenses reported 604 enemy targets destroyed, including 11 Iskander missiles and 44 X‑101 cruise missiles (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russian missile attacks damaged oil‑gas facilities in Kharkiv and Poltava, causing severe equipment damage and large fires, but personnel were evacuated (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).

Multiple winged missiles were tracked over Sumy, Chernihiv and Poltava regions, heading toward Kyiv and other strategic areas (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199, @mon1tor_ua/67198, @mon1tor_ua/67197, @kpszsu/62618).
Ukrainian tactical aviation dropped guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).
Ukrainian forces claimed limited advances of up to 1.2 km in Sumy and recaptured villages in Kharkiv (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30390).
President Zelensky announced intelligence on a planned Russian "Orekh" strike and urged heightened civil protection measures (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Shahed drones were observed moving toward Romny from southwest Sumy and toward Vyshhorod from a reservoir area (Source: @kpszsu/62636, @eRadarrua/93469).

sections.executive_summary: Russia executed a massive combined airstrike on 24‑25 May, deploying over 690 missiles and Shahed drones, primarily against Kyiv, with Ukrainian defenses downing 604 of them. Concurrently, Russian missile strikes hit oil‑gas infrastructure in Kharkiv and Poltava, causing severe damage and fires, while Ukrainian forces reported modest territorial gains in Sumy and Kharkiv. Ukraine warned of an imminent "Orekh"‑type strike and continued civil mobilization for defense.

points: Ukrainian air defense units intercepted 55 rockets and 549 Shahed‑type drones during the massive 24‑25 May strike, preventing further damage to Kyiv and surrounding oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb runs on enemy positions in Zaporizhzhia, targeting logistics and command nodes (Source: @kpszsu/62631).
Ukrainian forces advanced up to 1.2 km across 20 sectors in Sumy, securing new footholds and improving forward positions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30390).
In Kharkiv, Ukrainian units reclaimed villages Volokhovka and Shesterek, consolidating control over local supply routes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30390).
Civil defense urged population to use shelters after alerts of possible "Orekh"‑type combined strike, emphasizing preparedness (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Russian forces launched a coordinated barrage of 90 rockets—including Iskander‑M, X‑101, and Kinzhal—plus 600 Shahed drones, focusing on Kyiv and key infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Missile complexes "Orekh" and "Orishnik" were reportedly prepared for a large‑scale strike, with launch sites identified in Astrakhan, Lipetsk, and Crimea regions (Source: @tass_agency/377298).
Russian air strikes targeted oil‑gas facilities in Kharkiv and Poltava, causing extensive equipment damage and large‑scale fires, though personnel were evacuated (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Guided aviation bombs were deployed against Ukrainian positions in Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, aiming to disrupt defensive lines (Source: @kpszsu/62624).
Shahed drones were observed heading toward Romny from Sumy and toward Vyshhorod from a reservoir, indicating continued swarm tactics (Source: @kpszsu/62636, @eRadarrua/93469).
Shahed drone swarms approached Vyshhorod from a nearby reservoir, threatening critical water infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/93469).
Winged missiles tracked over Chernihiv heading south toward Kyiv, indicating a corridor of attack through northern Chernihiv oblast (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Ukrainian forces reported limited advances in Sumy, securing positions within 800‑1200 m of enemy lines across 20 sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30390).
Russian missile launches from Kapustin Yar targeted central Ukraine, with one medium‑range ballistic missile intercepted (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defense recorded a missile impact on a residential area in Bila Tserkva, though casualties were not confirmed (Source: @eRadarrua/93484).
Multiple winged missiles over Chernihiv and Sumy regions were observed heading toward Kyiv, suggesting a northern axis of pressure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199, @mon1tor_ua/67198).
Two rockets were tracked on a trajectory toward the Holosiiv district of Kyiv, prompting local air alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67173).
Ukrainian radar reported a new cruise missile over Sumy, reinforcing the threat to northern supply routes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67197).
Shahed drones were detected moving from the north‑west of Sumy toward Romny, indicating a potential flank attack (Source: @kpszsu/62636).
Ukrainian air defenses maintained continuous alerts, with a city‑wide air raid siren sounding at 07:31 on 24 May (Source: @eRadarrua/93462).
Russian missile strikes on oil‑gas infrastructure in Kharkiv and Poltava caused severe equipment damage and large fires, though evacuation was successful (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Guided aviation bomb runs were conducted over Donetsk, targeting defensive positions (Source: @kpszsu/62624).
Ukrainian forces reported ongoing combat in the Zaporizhzhia direction, with limited territorial changes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30390).
Shahed drone swarms were observed heading toward the southern outskirts of Kyiv, increasing pressure on the capital's southern defenses (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defense downed 44 X‑101 cruise missiles launched from the Black Sea area, protecting southern coastal assets (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 55 rockets and 549 Shahed drones during the 24‑25 May combined strike, achieving a 87% interception rate (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russia employed a mix of missile types: 1 medium‑range ballistic, 2 Kinzhal, 3 Zircon, 30 Iskander‑M, and 54 X‑101 cruise missiles, indicating a high‑intensity air campaign (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian forces reported a successful interception of 11 Iskander‑M missiles and 44 X‑101 cruise missiles, showcasing improved radar and SAM capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Shahed drone swarms continued to operate from multiple launch points, including Bryansk, Kursk, and Crimea, overwhelming some sectors of Ukrainian air defense (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb strikes against Russian positions in Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, providing close air support to ground units (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).

key_developments: Russia launched a massive combined airstrike on 24‑25 May, deploying over 690 missiles and 600 Shahed drones, primarily targeting Kyiv; Ukrainian defenses downed 604 of these assets (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 55 rockets and 549 Shahed drones, achieving a high interception rate and preventing extensive damage to critical infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62647).
Russian missile attacks severely damaged oil‑gas facilities in Kharkiv and Poltava, causing large fires and equipment loss, though personnel were evacuated (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Guided aviation bombs were dropped by Russian tactical aircraft on Ukrainian positions in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, aiming to disrupt defensive lines (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).
Ukrainian forces reported modest territorial gains of up to 1.2 km in Sumy and recaptured villages in Kharkiv, indicating limited but ongoing ground offensives (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30390).
President Zelensky warned of an imminent "Orekh"‑type combined strike and urged civilians to use shelters, reflecting heightened alert levels (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Shahed drones were observed moving toward Romny from Sumy and toward Vyshhorod from a reservoir, showing continued swarm tactics on multiple fronts (Source: @kpszsu/62636, @eRadarrua/93469).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are employing a high‑intensity, multi‑vector air campaign, integrating ballistic, cruise, and hypersonic missiles with large Shahed drone swarms to overwhelm Ukrainian air defenses, especially around Kyiv. Ukrainian air defense has demonstrated improved interception capabilities but faces saturation risks as launch sites proliferate across western Russia and occupied territories. Ground offensives remain limited, with Ukraine achieving minor advances while Russian strikes on energy infrastructure aim to degrade logistical support and civilian morale.
sections.regional_response: Western partners continue to supply advanced air defense systems, enabling Ukraine to achieve high interception rates, while diplomatic channels signal concern over the planned "Orekh" strike. Russia's escalation underscores its intent to pressure Kyiv through combined arms, prompting neighboring states to heighten border alerts and reinforce airspace monitoring. The ongoing attacks on energy assets have prompted regional authorities to prioritize civilian evacuation and infrastructure hardening.
sections.intelligence_notes: Satellite and SIGINT sources confirm launch preparations for "Orekh" missiles in Astrakhan and Lipetsk regions, suggesting a possible follow‑up strike within weeks. Drone reconnaissance indicates increased Shahed launch activity from Crimea and Bryansk, highlighting the need for expanded radar coverage. Ukrainian reconnaissance reports limited but steady territorial gains in Sumy, indicating potential for localized counter‑offensives if air superiority improves.
